text.title: Risk Register Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules — every register must satisfy all five.

rule.statement: Every register row MUST have a unique stable ID matching `R-\d{3,}`. IDs are never reused after closure.
rule.rationale: Stable IDs make audit-trail history, cross-document references, and post-mortem citations possible.

rule.statement: Every row MUST carry: id, description, category, p, i, score, strategy, owner, trigger, evidence, status, last_reviewed.
rule.rationale: Missing columns make the register incomplete and the validator rejects partial rows; this avoids 'half-managed' risks.

rule.statement: Every score (P, I) MUST point to a source artefact (file path, ADR, base-rate row); empty evidence fields are rejected.
rule.rationale: Evidence-less scores converge to Medium and lose discrimination value.

rule.statement: Status MUST be one of {active, closed_materialized, closed_passed, merged}. Free-text statuses are rejected.
rule.rationale: A bounded enum makes the register diffable, queryable, and audit-friendly.

rule.statement: Rows are never deleted. Closure happens via status change; merged rows reference the surviving id.
rule.rationale: Deletion destroys audit trail and lessons-learned pool.
